subject: Sociology
title: Education as Social Control
description: One-minute skill: Education as Social Control.

hook.text: Mandatory attendance, uniforms, standardized tests — school looks a lot like a system of control.

buildup.text: Critical theorists argue schools discipline bodies and minds to produce compliant workers and citizens.

discovery.text: Michel Foucault compared schools to prisons: both use surveillance, schedules, and exams to shape behavior.

twist.text: Schools also liberate. Literacy empowers dissent. Education has fueled every social movement in modern history.

climax.text: Education is paradoxical: it controls and empowers simultaneously, depending on who designs and accesses it.

punchline.text: Schools both cage and free the mind.

quiz.question: How did Foucault view schools?
options: As similar to prisons in using surveillance and discipline
